name: Couscous
recipeIngredient: 4 Medium-sized carrots, peeled and cut into 3/4-inch segments
3 small Turnips, peeled and cut into 3/4-inch cubes
2 Italian sweet peppers, deseeded and cut into 1/4 inch-thick rings
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the chickpeas overnight in at least four times their volume of water in a nonreactive bowl.
2. Heat the olive oil in a heavy-bottomed, 5- to 7-quart pot over medium heat.
3. Cut the lamb into 1-inch cubes and brown them (in batches if necessary) on all sides until golden brown. Transfer the meat to a warm platter.
4. Reduce the temperature to low and sauté the onions for 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
5. Add the lamb, boiling water, lemon juice, tomatoes, cinnamon, nutmeg, bay leaf, mint, salt, and pepper to the pot. Stir, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes.
6. Drain the chickpeas and stir them into the lamb mixture. Simmer, covered, for an additional 45 minutes until the lamb is tender.
7. Peel the carrots and turnips, then cut the carrots into 3/4-inch segments and the turnips into 3/4-inch cubes. When step 6 is completed, stir these ingredients into the pot containing the lamb. Simmer, covered, for an additional 15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
8. While the vegetables are simmering in step 7, prepare the couscous. Place the pellets in a 2- to 3-quart pan and pour cold tap water over them until they are completely submerged by at least 1 inch of water. Allow to soak for 10 minutes.
9. Pour out the water from the soaking pan and return only the soaked pellets. Slowly strain two cups of broth from the large pot into the couscous, stirring constantly. Bring this mixture to a simmer and cover. Cook over low heat for 5 minutes.
10. Slice the zucchini into 3-inch pieces and the peppers into 1/4 inch-thick rings. When step 7 is completed, stir in both ingredients and continue to simmer until vegetables are tender, about 10 more minutes.
11. Turn off the heat under the couscous pellets as soon as the zucchini-and-pepper mixture starts to simmer in step 10. Fluff the pellets with a fork, cover the pan, and let it stand for an additional 8 minutes before serving.
12. Arrange the couscous pellets in the center of a warm, large serving platter. Using a slotted spoon, remove the meat and vegetables from the pot and arrange them around the mound.
Serves 6 to 8.
